input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to specify values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current `AppTheme` and picks the matching value. When the user toggles system dark mode, the UI updates **automatically** — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or setup method:

// For Color properties (shorthand):
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#F0F0F0"));

// For any property (general form):
myPage.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

myButton.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Tips:**

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Resource dictionaries** | Define colors in `App.xaml` as `<Color>` resources and reference them with `AppThemeBinding` for reuse across pages. |
| **Detect current theme** | `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified`. |
| **React to changes** | Subscribe to `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged` if you need custom logic beyond UI bindings. |
| **Force a theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to override the system setting. |

Both approaches are equivalent at runtime — XAML is more declarative, C# gives you dynamic control. Pick whichever fits your workflow.
